content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 - SACRAMENTO 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO 95814

Technical Assistance Administration ... Executive Legal ... Enforcement ... Conflict of Inter.
916 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-5444

July 20, 1977

77-07-054

Hugh R. Coffin
Assistant City Attorney
City of Newport Beach
3300 Newport Boulevard
Newport Beach, California 92663

Dear Mr. Coffin:

This is in response to your letter to the Commission dated
13 July 1977. The Commission will provide written advice pur-
suant to Government Code §83114(b) and its Regulations only
if:

"The identity of the person whose duties are in
question shall be provided with the request for
written advice. If the request for written advice
is submitted by the representative of several
persons similarly situated, the identity of at
least one such person shall be provided with the
request."
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1)

Accordingly, in order to take advantage of the immunity granted
by 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(q); you must identify the Planning
Commissioner involved in your request.

However, I think it appropriate to make the following
general observations as they may go some way toward resolving
the question you pose.

Assuming the Commissioner "owns directly, indirectly or
beneficially a 20% or greater interest in an environmental
planning and landscape architecture firm, his pro rata share of
the gross receipts of the firm constitute "income" to him.
See, Government Code §82010. The Irvine Company thus becomes
a "source of income" to him within the meaning of §87103(d).

2-B-3

Mr. Hugh R. Coffin                                                 Page 2
July 20, 1977


    Government Code §§87100 and 87103 therefore require that the
Commissioner disqualify himself from making, participating in the
making, or using his official position to influence any decision
which will "foreseeably have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally," on...
the Irvine Company. §87103. The possibly unused facial question
here is whether all or only some of the decisions cited
in your letter will have a "material financial effect" on Irvine
Company. The Commission has adopted Regulations defining "material
financial effect". 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18702, a copy of which
is enclosed. You should be guided by the Regulation in determin-
ing the existence of this effect on a case-by-case basis.

    Thank you for writing the Commission. If you have further
questions concerning this or any matter, please feel free to
call on me at (916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa

Enclosure

CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
714-644-3201

July 13, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Conflict of Interest Division

Gentlemen:

By this letter we are requesting an informal written opinion
regarding a possible conflict of interest of a Planning
Commissioner of the City of Newport Beach.

The Commissioner in question is a shareholder of an environmental planning and landscape architectural firm which provides
services to both public and private entities. The firm lists
The Irvine Company as one of its current clients. Currently,
it is performing services for The Irvine Company in the City
of Newport Beach and on three projects in the City of Irvine.
The income provided to the Planning Commissioner exceeds
$1,000.00 per year in his share of his company's income from
Irvine Company contracts. From time to time projects sponsored
by The Irvine Company or projects involving Irvine Company
land, sponsored by others, are brought before the Planning
Commission of the City of Newport Beach for various forms of
discretionary approval.

The question is whether or not this Planning Commissioner may
participate in decisions before the Planning Commission when
The Irvine Company is the applicant or the application involves
the development and use of lands owned by The Irvine Company.

Your review of these facts and informal opinion as to whether
or not a conflict of interest exists in regards to this
particular Planning Commissioner would be greatly appreciated.



City Hall • 3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach, California 92663

July 13, 1977
Page Two


Should you require additional information or have any questions
regarding this matter, please contact me and we can discuss
the matter further.

Very truly yours,


HUGH R. COFFIN
Assistant City Attorney


HRC:yz

The Commission will provide written advice pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) and its Regulations only if: "The identity of the person whose duties are in question shall be provided with the request for written advice. If the request for written advice is submitted by the representative of several persons similarly situated, the identity of at least one such person shall be provided with the request." 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1) Accordingly, in order to take advantage of the immunity granted by 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(q); you must identify the Planning Commissioner involved in your request. However, I think it appropriate to make the following general observations as they may go some way toward resolving the question you pose. Assuming the Commissioner "owns directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10% interest or greater" in the environmental planning and landscape architecture firm, his pro rata share of the gross receipts of the firm constitute "income" to him. See, Government Code §82030. The Irvine Company thus becomes a "source of income" to him within the meaning of §87103(c). Mr. Hugh R. Coffin July 20, 1977 Government Code §§87100 and 87103 therefore require that the Commissioner disqualify himself from making, participating in the making, or using his official position to influence any decision which will "foreseeably have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally," on... The Irvine Company. §87103. The possibly unresolved factual question here is whether all or only some of the decisions cited in your letter will have a "material financial effect" on Irvine Company. The Commission has adopted Regulations defining "material financial effect", 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18702, a copy of which is enclosed. You should be guided by the Regulation in determining the existence of this effect on a case-by-case basis. Thank you for writing the Commission. If you have further questions concerning this or any matter, please feel free to call on me at (916)322-6444. Sincerely, [Signature] Kenneth W.
